riders_group_posts.text: $1,420 Mukilteo, WA — Axis Fireball 1750 Brand New, never used and purchased new directly from Axis.
new scootpump foil board looks sweet on the Fireball 1750. [See more]

comments_detail.text: friends don't let friends ride scooters!
Do you actually attach a board on top of that rail? Or go full minimalist?

riders_group_posts.text: following on from the pumping question - here is my hot take after 10 days straight on the surge 1010. It will improve your pumping immensely. [See more]

comments_detail.text: Can I ask you how much you weigh.

I've been swapping back and forth between the Surge and the Fireball a lot the last days trying to decide where my heart lies… and for wing, parawing and Foil Drive action it is Surge all the way. The fireball is more efficient without a doubt and leap… [See more]

riders_group_posts.product_intel: Surge 1010 praised for pumping improvement; Surge preferred over Fireball for wing/parawing/Foil Drive; Fireball more efficient

riders_group_posts.text: Which wings are easy to pump on SUP? I'm 175lbs and currently SUP foil, para wing (+dw), and dockstart on the 1300v1. I also have an Art Pro 1201 and a 999v2… but I cannot consistently pump either of them. I hear that the 1201 is supposed to be a pump machine, but as soon as I loose speed it'll drop out hard on me. I also can't flat water popup on it, but the 1300 is starting to feel real big in the surf and slow in the bumps. Is there a larger Surge coming… [See more]
riders_group_posts.discipline: SUP foil, para wing, DW, dockstart

comments_detail.text: Nick what happened? You did over a minute on my set up? Was it the set up or the instructor?

The 1201 is a great sup surf foil that pups amazing with the right stab. Your problem is too small a stab. Not sure why but on the sup I can only pump for 2-3 waves with the skinny 45 but with the progressive 375 I can connect 10+ waves. A large skinn… [See more]

riders_group_posts.product_intel: Art Pro 1201 pumping issues often stab-related (too small stab). Progressive 375 stab recommended over skinny 45 for SUP pumping. Question about larger Surge.

riders_group_posts.text: 80 kgs, looking to learn parawing on 90 litre midlength. Surge 1010 or Spitfire 1030/1100? I have the Surge 950, even if it gets up real easy I think it is on the small side for me to learn parawing? [See more]

comments_detail.text: The spitfire is an awesome wing wing and has been for a few years now but the Surge has the benefit of several years more knowledge and R and D and is one of the new high camber foil designs. The early starting, pump and glide and of course playfulness… [See more]

1010 Surge is my light wind weapon for wingfoiling. I'm 85 kg. I can't comment on Parawing specifically, but if your board gets you going easily enough on your 950, then Surge 1010 could be the ideal wing. I had the Spitfire 1030 previously and like it… [See more]

riders_group_posts.product_intel: Surge 1010 vs Spitfire 1030 debate for parawing. Community consensus leans toward Surge due to newer R&D and high-camber design. Surge 950 considered too small for 80kg parawing learner.

riders_group_posts.text: Catching waves with lower motor pod in efoil position on axis integrated mast using fusion. Still can pump and stay on the bumps. Small 1-2ft shore break type of da… [See more] (with Austin Keen)

comments_detail.text: Can you feel a lot of drag tho? I've never even considered tryna ride motor off? You think a Stoke Boost would be similar?

riders_group_posts.sentiment: positive, showcasing efoil motor-off riding capability
riders_group_posts.product_intel: Axis integrated mast enables motor-off wave riding. Fusion motor pod used. Stoke Boost comparison raised.

riders_group_posts.text: Hi all, I'm 85 kg and mainly use my setup for wingfoiling and downwind runs with the wing, mostly on lakes. I consider myself an intermediate winger. My current quiver is: ART V2 999 for 14–25mph and ART V2 939 for strong wind days (20-25mph). The 999 is my daily driver (I use it 90% of the time). After an initial adjustment period, I've real… [See more]

comments_detail.text: For super light wind like that, also consider your board! I switched from a biscuit board to a 7' F One and it's been awesome. Accelerates, planes, bounces off water. Our 'windy' days are usually have 10-14 knots and it's made great days out of nothin… [See more]

I'm 85kg. Used to ride 1099 and 999v1, 939v2, 1030 spitfire, etc. 1030 was my latest light wind foil for winging with 6.5m and 94ltr 23" wide board, until I made the switch to Surges - 1010, 890, 830, etc. Surge 1010 is my light wind weapon, and the mo… [See more]

riders_group_posts.product_intel: ART V2 999 popular daily driver at 85kg. Board choice matters for light wind. Strong advocacy for Surge 1010 as light wind weapon upgrade from traditional wings.

riders_group_posts.text: Hybrid Carbon foil boards — Was wondering anyone in the know if the green sea foam color Axis hybrid carbon foil boards were designed by Frank that did the darts or another shaper? Anyone using them for parawing

comments_detail.text: Hi Doug, I have para-winged the Hybrid 120l in lightish winds. If you have one it works ok but not as well as a more dedicated board like the Mini Dart (which jumps out of the water with amazing ease). However if you are looking for one board to Sup fo… [See more]
Pre Frank's involvement. I designed the Hybrid and Blasts

riders_group_posts.product_intel: Adrian Roper confirms he designed the Hybrid and Blast boards (pre-Frank). Hybrid 120l works for parawing but Mini Dart is better dedicated option.

riders_group_posts.text: Hi all. Has anyone got experience using PNGv2 1200 for wake or wing foiling? I'd like to get one for dock starts but would like to know what it's like for other winging and wake foiling. I'm a beginner at dock starts. Intermediate wing foiler and tow foiler. I use art pro v2 999 and HPS1050. I'm 77kg

comments_detail.text: I have used it for both. Wing in sub 10 knot wind. It's very slow and turns have to be deliberate and slow. Wake is fine. You can 15 wakes back but same thing. It likes to go straight and is super hard to turn.

Wide wings are a PITA to control on boat wakes, too wide to control well. Even the PNG 1150 was hard to ride on a boat wake. The couple times I've tried wider wings it wasn't worth it. Now to pull up in the flats and drop the handle to just pump is… [See more]

riders_group_posts.product_intel: PNG 1200 — good for low wind but turns poorly and goes straight. Wide wings difficult for wake riding.
